rationale: go build ./... is clean on d713a0b7. go test ./... shows every package spec 112 touches (cmd/mindspec, internal/config, internal/complete, internal/panel) GREEN, along with all other packages except two: internal/harness and internal/instruct. Both are the two KNOWN pre-existing failures named in the BRIEF. I verified both are unrelated to 112's diff by checking out the merge-base 2f894401 in a detached worktree (/tmp/spec112-mergebase-check) and re-running: (1) internal/instruct TestRun_IdleNoBeads fails identically at merge-base with the exact same signature — it renders the 'Multiple Active Specs' template instead of 'No Active Work' because the real repo currently has specs 110/111/112 in progress (test isn't sandboxed from live .mindspec state; this is the tracked z4ps test-isolation leak). (2) internal/harness fails identically at merge-base too — both runs die via 'panic: test timed out after 10m0s' inside the package's live-agent scenario tests (ClaudeCodeAgent.Run spawning a real claude-code subprocess); on the branch it was killed mid TestLLM_SpecToIdle, at merge-base mid TestLLM_ApprovalGateDiscovery — the specific test varies by real subprocess timing, but the package-level 10-minute-timeout-panic failure mode is identical and pre-existing. No new regression introduced by 112.

findings.detail: internal/harness and internal/instruct fail on both d713a0b7 and merge-base 2f894401 with matching signatures (10m package timeout inside live-agent scenario tests; TestRun_IdleNoBeads 'Multiple Active Specs' vs 'No Active Work' due to unsandboxed real-repo state). Confirmed pre-existing, not introduced by spec 112.
All packages spec 112 modifies (cmd/mindspec, internal/config, internal/complete, internal/panel) pass cleanly; go build ./... succeeds with no errors.
